title: The Magicians of Caprona - Chapter 12 (Fragment 3)
text:
They walked around to the front of the papers and looked at them curiously. The top one said:

Report of Campaign at 08.00 hours. Summary: Troops advancing on all fronts to repel invasion. Heavy Artillery and Reservists moving up in support. Pisan front reports heavy losses. Fleet sighted—Pisan?—steaming for mouth of Voltava .

“We’re at war!” said Tonino. “Why?”

“Because the Duchess has got us, of course,” said Angelica. “And our families daren’t make war-spells. Tonino, we must get out . We must tell them where the words to the Angel are!”

“But why does the Duchess want Caprona beaten?” Tonino said.

“I don’t know,” said Angelica. “There’s something wrong about her, I know that. Aunt Bella said there was an awful fuss when the Duke decided to marry her. Nobody likes her.”

“Let’s see if we can open the window,” said Tonino. He set off along the piano again. do-ti-so-fa-me-re—

“Quiet!” said Angelica.

Tonino discovered that, if he put each foot down very slowly, the notes did not sound. He was halfway along the keyboard, and Angelica had one foot stretched out to follow, when they heard someone opening the door again. There was no time to be careful. Angelica fled back to the desk. Tonino, with a terrible discord, scrambled across the black notes and squeezed behind the music on the stand.

He was only just in time. When he looked—he was standing with his feet and head sideways, like an Ancient Egyptian—the Duke of Caprona himself was standing in front of the desk. Tonino thought the Duke seemed both puzzled and sad. He was tapping the Report of Campaign against his teeth and did not seem to notice Angelica standing between the Punch and the Judy on his desk, although Angelica’s eyes were blinking against the glitter from the Duke’s buttons.

“But I didn’t declare war!” the Duke said to himself. “I was watching that puppet show. How could I—?” He sighed and bit the Report worriedly between two rows of big shiny teeth. “Is my mind going?” he asked. He seemed to be talking to Angelica. She had the sense not to answer. “I must go and ask Lucrezia,” the Duke said. He flung the Report down at Angelica’s feet and hurried out of the study.

Tonino slid cautiously down the piano lid onto the keys again— kerpling . Angelica was now standing at the end of the piano, pointing at the window. She was speechless with horror.

Tonino looked—and for a moment he was as frightened as Angelica. There was a brown monster glaring at him through the glass, wide-faced, wide-eyed and shaggy. The thing had eyes like yellow lamps.

Faintly, through the glass, came a slightly irritable request to pull himself together and open the window.

“Benvenuto!” shouted Tonino.

“Oh—it’s only a cat,” Angelica quavered. “How terrible it must feel to be a mouse!”

“Just a cat!” Tonino said scornfully. “That’s Benvenuto.” He tried to explain to Benvenuto that it was not easy to open windows when you were nine inches high.

Benvenuto’s impatient answer was to shove Tonino’s latest magic exercise book in front of Tonino’s mind’s eye, open at almost the first page.

“Oh, thanks,” Tonino said, rather ashamed. There were three opening-spells on that page, and none of them had stuck in his head. He chose the easiest, shut his eyes so that he could read the imaginary page more clearly, and sang the spell.

Gently and easily, the window swung open, letting in a gust of cold wind. And Benvenuto came in with the wind, almost as lightly. As Benvenuto trod gently up the scale towards him, Tonino had another moment when he knew how mice felt. Then he forgot it in the gladness of seeing Benvenuto. He stretched his arms wide to rub behind Benvenuto’s horny ears. Benvenuto put his sticky black nose to Tonino’s face, and they both stood, delighted, holding down a long humming discord on the piano.

vocabulary.definition: To move forward in a purposeful way.
vocabulary.example: Summary: Troops advancing on all fronts to repel invasion.

vocabulary.definition: Large-caliber guns used in warfare on land.
vocabulary.example: Heavy Artillery and Reservists moving up in support.

vocabulary.definition: An organized course of action to achieve a particular goal, especially in a political, military, or commercial context.
vocabulary.example:
The top one said:

Report of Campaign at 08.

vocabulary.definition: In a careful way to avoid danger or mistakes.
vocabulary.example: Tonino slid cautiously down the piano lid onto the keys again— kerpling .

vocabulary.definition: To announce something publicly or officially.
vocabulary.example: “But I didn’t declare war!

vocabulary.definition: Very pleased and happy.
vocabulary.example: Benvenuto put his sticky black nose to Tonino’s face, and they both stood, delighted, holding down a long humming discord on the piano.

vocabulary.definition: Lack of harmony between notes; a harsh or unpleasant combination of sounds.
vocabulary.example: Tonino, with a terrible discord, scrambled across the black notes and squeezed behind the music on the stand.

vocabulary.definition: A display of unnecessary or excessive concern or activity.
vocabulary.example: Aunt Bella said there was an awful fuss when the Duke decided to marry her.

vocabulary.definition: In a kind and tender manner; softly.
vocabulary.example: Gently and easily, the window swung open, letting in a gust of cold wind.

vocabulary.definition: A shining brightness, especially reflected light.
vocabulary.example: He was tapping the Report of Campaign against his teeth and did not seem to notice Angelica standing between the Punch and the Judy on his desk, although Angelica’s eyes were blinking against the glitter from the Duke’s buttons.

vocabulary.definition: A brief, strong rush of wind.
vocabulary.example: Gently and easily, the window swung open, letting in a gust of cold wind.

vocabulary.definition: Having a hard keratinous covering, like horns; tough and rough.
vocabulary.example: He stretched his arms wide to rub behind Benvenuto’s horny ears.

vocabulary.definition: An intense feeling of fear, shock, or disgust.
vocabulary.example: She was speechless with horror.

vocabulary.definition: Eager or restless due to delay or opposition.
vocabulary.example: Benvenuto’s impatient answer was to shove Tonino’s latest magic exercise book in front of Tonino’s mind’s eye, open at almost the first page.

vocabulary.definition: Easily annoyed or provoked.
vocabulary.example: Faintly, through the glass, came a slightly irritable request to pull himself together and open the window.

vocabulary.definition: Confused or uncertain.
vocabulary.example: Tonino thought the Duke seemed both puzzled and sad.

vocabulary.definition: To shake or tremble in speaking, especially from nervousness or emotion.
vocabulary.example: “Oh—it’s only a cat,” Angelica quavered.

vocabulary.definition: To drive back or resist an attack or effort.
vocabulary.example: Summary: Troops advancing on all fronts to repel invasion.

vocabulary.definition: A member of a military reserve force.
vocabulary.example: Heavy Artillery and Reservists moving up in support.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows contempt or disdain.
vocabulary.example: ” Tonino said scornfully.

vocabulary.definition: To move hurriedly or clumsily using hands and feet.
vocabulary.example: Tonino, with a terrible discord, scrambled across the black notes and squeezed behind the music on the stand.

vocabulary.definition: To let out a long deep breath expressing sadness, relief, or tiredness.
vocabulary.example: ” He sighed and bit the Report worriedly between two rows of big shiny teeth.

vocabulary.definition: Adhering or holding fast like glue or syrup.
vocabulary.example: Benvenuto put his sticky black nose to Tonino’s face, and they both stood, delighted, holding down a long humming discord on the piano.

vocabulary.definition: A brief statement or account of the main points of something.
vocabulary.example: Summary: Troops advancing on all fronts to repel invasion.
